blocks.text: Mr. Adam Cook, Director of Operations, Ramos, Gill and Garcia
Re: Distribution Network Review
Dear Mr. Cook:

Thank you for the courtesy you and your staff extended during our visit. This letter confirms that {{fact:f:E-1998-001.client}} has engaged Cindergrove Advisors Inc to conduct a review of your distribution network. The purpose of the engagement is to examine the flow of product from your plants through your warehouses to your customers, to identify where cost and delay enter that flow, and to recommend practical changes that your own people can carry out and sustain.

Our approach will be straightforward. We will begin with your shipping and freight records, walk each warehouse in person, and interview the supervisors responsible for scheduling and carrier selection. Findings will be reduced to writing before any recommendation is made, and we will review our observations with you at each step, so that nothing in the final report comes to you as a surprise.

Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, will direct the work and will be your day-to-day contact. She will be assisted in the field by Andrea Diaz, Consultant, who will handle the site visits and the assembly of the working papers. I will review all written work before it leaves this office and will remain available to you throughout the engagement.

Fieldwork will commence on {{fact:f:E-1998-001.start}}, and we expect the engagement to run approximately four months. Our fee for the work described above is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-1998-001.fee}}, payable in monthly installments upon invoice, plus reasonable out-of-pocket travel expenses billed at cost. No expense of consequence will be incurred without your prior approval.

If these terms are acceptable, please sign in the space provided below and return one copy to our office by fax or by mail. We appreciate the confidence Ramos, Gill and Garcia has placed in our firm, and we look forward to the work. Very truly yours,
Kickoff Memo: Distribution Network Review
To: Andrea Diaz. From: Kimberly Price. Re: Distribution Network Review for Ramos, Gill and Garcia.

The engagement letter has been countersigned and returned, and fieldwork begins on {{fact:f:E-1998-001.start}}. Our objective is to give {{fact:f:E-1998-001.client}} a clear picture of what its distribution network costs, where the delays occur, and what to change first. Our contact is Adam Cook, Director of Operations. Anything addressed to his office goes out over my signature until I say otherwise.

items: Freight and shipping records: assemble the carrier invoices and bills of lading provided by the client; Andrea Diaz to build the working schedules.
Warehouse walk-throughs: on-site observation at each location, with layouts, staffing, and dock practices noted on a common form.
Interviews: scheduling supervisors and the staff responsible for carrier selection, conducted at the client's offices.
Baseline analysis: reduce the records to a cost-per-shipment view suitable for discussion with the client.

blocks.text: Next steps: Andrea Diaz will confirm the site visit arrangements with Adam Cook's office this week, and I will draft the interview guide for review before we schedule anything. Please put open questions in writing before we are on site; I would rather answer them here than in front of the client.
Distribution Network Review
Prepared for {{fact:f:E-1998-001.client}} by Cindergrove Advisors Inc. Presented by Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant.
Engagement Background

items: Fieldwork commenced {{fact:f:E-1998-001.start}}
Sponsor: Adam Cook, Director of Operations, Ramos, Gill and Garcia
Scope: flow of product from plants through warehouses to customers
Emphasis on freight cost, delay, and documentation practice

blocks.text: Work Completed to Date

items: Freight and shipping records assembled and tabulated
Warehouse walk-throughs completed by Andrea Diaz
Interviews with scheduling supervisors under way
Working papers current and reviewed weekly

blocks.text: Preliminary Observations

items: Carrier selection varies by warehouse; no common rate reference in use
Split shipments appear more often than order patterns would require
Documentation practices differ from one location to the next

blocks.text: Next Steps

items: Complete supervisor interviews and the baseline cost analysis
Review findings with the client before the steering committee meets
Issue an interim findings memorandum for comment

blocks.text: Meeting Minutes: Distribution Network Review Working Session
Working session held at the offices of {{fact:f:E-1998-001.client}}. Minutes prepared by Andrea Diaz and circulated to all attendees for correction.

items: Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc
Andrea Diaz, Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc
Adam Cook, Director of Operations, Ramos, Gill and Garcia

blocks.text: Kimberly Price opened the session with a summary of fieldwork completed to date. The warehouse walk-throughs are finished, and the freight records furnished by the client have been tabulated. She reported that the interviews with scheduling supervisors are proceeding on schedule and that the baseline analysis will follow once the records are settled.

Adam Cook raised a concern regarding carrier invoices that could not be matched to bills of lading. Andrea Diaz confirmed that a number of invoices remain unmatched and stated that she has not yet determined whether the gap lies in the records themselves or in the filing; she will report on the question once she has traced it rather than offer a guess. The group agreed that the matter should be settled before any baseline figures are presented.

rows: Reconcile the unmatched carrier invoices against bills of lading and report the cause
Furnish warehouse staffing rosters to the engagement team
Circulate the draft baseline analysis for internal review

blocks.text: Status Report: Distribution Network Review
Prepared for {{fact:f:E-1998-001.client}} by Kimberly Price, Senior Consultant, Cindergrove Advisors Inc.
Status Summary

The Distribution Network Review remains on schedule. Fieldwork is complete, the baseline cost analysis is in draft, and the engagement remains within the fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-1998-001.fee}} set out in our engagement letter. We see no reason at this time to revise either the timetable or the budget, and we will advise you promptly if that changes.
Accomplishments This Period

items: Completed the interviews with scheduling and carrier-selection supervisors.
Andrea Diaz reconciled the outstanding carrier invoices against bills of lading; the records are now complete enough to support the analysis, and the cause of the earlier gaps has been documented.
Drafted the baseline cost-per-shipment analysis and placed it in internal review.

blocks.text: Upcoming Work

items: Review the baseline analysis with the management of Ramos, Gill and Garcia.
Develop recommendations and test each one against the corrected records before it is put in writing.
Prepare the draft final report and the steering-committee presentation.

blocks.text: Risks

Certain warehouse staffing rosters have not yet been received; a further delay would push the staffing portion of the analysis into the next period. In addition, recommendations that touch carrier arrangements may require lead time to implement, and we will flag any such item as early as the analysis permits. Questions regarding this report may be directed to Kimberly Price. The next report will be issued in the ordinary course at the close of the coming period.
